title: phyllanthus pelas
intro: Phyllanthus pelas, a type of plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, is also known as Annona glabra or water chestnut tree, but is more commonly referred to as Pulasan or Phyllanthus amarus, in various cultures. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phyllanthus pelas include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate liver and kidney problems, and prevent the spread of certain diseases. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often prepared by boiling the leaves and roots in water.
